zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Hay of Errol - Countess of Huntly於大約1442年出生在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾亨特利時，她的父親Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles31歲，母親Beatrice Douglas14歲。她和Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland至少有2個兒子和6個女兒。她去世於1509年6月27日在英國蘇格蘭享年68歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Hay of Errol - Countess of Huntly于大约1442年出生在英国苏格兰阿爾迪史爾亨特利时，她的父亲Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles31岁，母亲Beatrice Douglas14岁。她和Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland至少有2个儿子和6个女儿。她去世于1509年6月27日在英国苏格兰享年68岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Hay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy called La Haie or La Haye (Old French haie ‘hedge, enclosure’, ‘forest for hunting deer and other animals’, a borrowing of the ancient Germanic word haga). Robert de Haia or de la Haye is known to have come from La Haye-du-Puits in Manche; he was the founder of Boxgrove Priory in Sussex (1123), and holder of the Honor of Halnaker (Sussex) and (by marriage) the barony of Kolswein (Lincolnshire). The Norman name was also taken to Ireland, where it has since flourished in the county of Wexford as Hay and Hayes . Elsewhere in Ireland the name usually has a native Irish origin, see below.

English: topographic name from Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg) or ‘forest fenced off for hunting’ (Old French haie); or else a habitational name from a place so called, such as Hay (in Herefordshire and Westmorland) or Hey in Scammonden (Yorkshire). It was no doubt sometimes synonymous with Hayward .
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah).

variants.name: High
meanings: English:
nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hay 3.
topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) height or hill’, from Middle English heigh ‘height, hilltop’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hight .

variants.name: Hai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Abdul, Syed, Arifa, Atta, Faiz, Hamid, Iftekhar, Iskandar, Mohamed, Mohammad, Mohannad.  Vietnamese Mui, Pham, Tran, Vo, Bui, Cao, Chau, Dinh, Duong, Nguyen, Sau, Vo Van.  Jewish Ilan, Ronen, Shlomo.  Chinese Chan, Chiming, Lung, Wai Fun, Yin.
Muslim (mainly the Indian subcontinent): from a personal name based on Arabic ḥayy ‘alive’. Al-Ḥayy ‘the Living’ is an attribute of Allah.
Jewish: variant of   Hay  .

variants.name: Dehay
meanings: French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the masculine form of Old French haye ‘hedge’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word (see Hay 9).
Altered form (also De Hay) of Dutch De Haij or De Haaij, which is probably of French origin (see above).

variants.name: Haig
meanings: Scottish and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named with Old Norse hagi ‘enclosure’, a word with cognates in most ancient Germanic languages. Compare Hay . The Haigs of Bemersyde in Berwickshire derive their name from the district of La Hague in Manche.
Scottish and Irish (Cavan): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Thaoig and Mac Thaidhg (see McCaig ).

variants.name: Hayman
meanings: English: topographic name probably from Middle English hai(e), hei(e) ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg), denoting a dweller by an enclosure (see Hay 2 ), or else a nickname for a ‘keeper of the enclosure’ (compare Hayward ).
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, high(e) (Old English hēah); see also Hay 3.
English: occupational name for a seller of hay, from Middle English hai, hei (Old English hēg, hīg).

variants.name: Hayes
meanings: Irish (Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAodha ‘descendant of Aodh’, a personal name meaning ‘fire’. Compare McCoy . In some cases especially in County Wexford, the surname is of English origin (see below), having been taken to Ireland by the Normans.
English: variant of Hay , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: topographic name from the plural form of Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (see Hay ), sometimes used as a collective noun for a farm, especially in Devon, where it is a frequent minor placename. Compare Hain .

variants.name: Haycock
meanings: English: from a pet form of the Middle English personal name Hai + coc; see Hay 4 and compare Cocke .

variants.name: Haylett
meanings: English (Norfolk): from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Hai (see Hay 4), from Hai + -el + -ot.

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Hay of Errol - Countess of Huntly was born about 1442, in Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles, was 31 and her mother, Beatrice Douglas, was 14. She had at least 2 sons and 6 daughters with Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland. She died on 27 June 1509, in Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 68, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Hay of Errol - Countess of Huntly est née environ 1442, à Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Beatrice Douglas, avait 14 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 6 filles avec Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland. Elle est décédée le 27 juin 1509, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterrée à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Hay of Errol - Countess of Huntly um 1442 in Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles 31 und ihre Mutter Beatrice Douglas 14. Sie hatte mit Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland mindestens 2 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 27. Juni 1509 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Hay of Errol - Countess of Huntly è nata il circa 1442 nel Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Beatrice Douglas, aveva 14 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 6 figlie con Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland. Morì il 27 giugno 1509, nel Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolta a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hay of Errol - Countess of Huntlyが約1442年にイギリススコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアハントリーで生まれた時，父Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peeblesは31歳，母Beatrice Douglasは14歳であった。彼女はSir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotlandとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と6人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1509年6月27日にイギリススコットランドで68歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hay of Errol - Countess of Huntly(이)가 대략 1442년 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르 헌틀리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrice Douglas(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1509년 6월 27일 영국 스코틀랜드에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Hay of Errol - Countess of Huntly nasceu aproximadamente 1442, em Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Beatrice Douglas, tinha 14 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 6 filhas com Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland. Ela faleceu em 27 de junho de 1509, em Escócia, United Kingdom, com 68 anos, e foi sepultada em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Hay of Errol - Countess of Huntly родилась около 1442 г. в местности хантли, абирдиншир, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Beatrice Douglas, 14. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 6 дочерей от Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland. Она умерла 27 июня 1509 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 68 лет и была похоронена в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Hay of Errol - Countess of Huntly nació aproximadamente 1442, en Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Beatrice Douglas, tenía 14 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 6 hijas con Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland. Murió el 27 de junio de 1509, en Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultada en Scotland.
